programmes.show_description: Connor Phillips is live through the night from the Salford Riviera with Midnight Mastermind, Wish You Were There, Midnight Caller and a huge Ravio 2 to end the week.

programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: Vernon unearths some long forgotten dance gems from the 90s... Expect tunes you haven't heard in ages to unlock those memories of the best decade in dance!
Welcome! It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Airdrum Anthems.
OJ's got your Good Friday morning sorted... with Jack Savoretti and new music!
Gary looks back on this day in music history in 'De Ja Woo'; there's the mid-morning music quiz Ten To The Top and Mike Pickering chooses his final two Tracks Of My Years.
Tony sets the bank holiday mood with three hours of the very best soul and disco music - including tracks from Chic, Olivia Dean, Diana Ross, MT Jones, Sister Sledge and more.
Mark Goodier presents his essential selection of the finest 'billion streamers' from the past six decades of rock and pop.
The Reverend Kate Bottley reflects on the loss, faith and hope found in the Easter story, with inspiring guests and beautiful music.
Angela Griffin has the perfect playlist for a Good Friday night, to help you unwind.

programmes.show_name: Romesh Ranganathan: For the Love of Hip Hop
programmes.show_description: Romesh talks to Cypress Hill's B-Real about their legendary album Black Sunday, rapping in Spanish and their concert with the London Symphony Orchestra at The Royal Albert Hall.
Tony's back with another jam packed super Soul selection of classics from across the decades including The Supremes, Kenni Burke, Sam and Dave, Lou Rawls and Patti Labelle.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1968.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1978.
James performing live at Shepherd's Bush Empire in 1997. Featuring She's A Star, Born of Frustration, Waltzing Along, Sit Down, Tomorrow, Lose Control and Laid.

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin has an Easter Unwinds Mix, with music from Eva Cassidy, Sugababes and The Cranberries.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from The Beatles, Marvin Gaye and Dionne Warwick. This week, the top three comes from 1969.
Wine expert Olly Smith chats about his debut novel Death By Noir. Plus, Dermot's dad's Grand National picks - and a wedding call, Dusty Springfield on vinyl and The Mystery Voice.
Rom's best pal, comedian and actor Tom Davis (Wonka, Murder in Successville, Paddington 2), joins Romesh live in the studio to chat about his new tour 'Spudgun'.
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma and enjoy a menu full of your favourite music, great conversation and a delicious dive into the BBC archives for dessert.
Rylan's joined by Fay Ripley to chat about the brand-new celebrity undercover series Celebrity Sabotage. Plus, Fay shares her RY Player, revealing her  favourite film and TV shows.
Shaun Keaveny with fabulous teatime tunes and your tales of the extraordinary and the everyday.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 80s with Gary Davies
programmes.show_description: It's Easter weekend! So here come two hours packed with egg-stended versions of iconic 80s hits - and the B-52s' Fred Schneider guests on My 80s, too!

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Fearne's remembering big moments from this week in the 90s, from the release of Blur's 'Song 2', to Anthea Turner's Tracey Island recreation! All soundtracked by 90s bangers.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: One hour of 90s tunes to add just a little bit of sunshine, from the likes of Supergrass, Sheryl Crow and Alanis Morissette.
